contents.value:
```yaml
key: value
nested:
  child: value
```
A mapping (dictionary/hash) of key-value pairs. Keys must be unique within a mapping. Mappings are the fundamental structure in YAML for associating names with values.

```yaml
items:
  - item1
  - item2
  - item3
```
A sequence (array/list) of ordered values. Each item starts with a dash `-` followed by a space. Sequences preserve insertion order.

```yaml
plain: hello world
single_quoted: 'no escapes'
double_quoted: "supports \n escapes"
```
A scalar is a single value — a string, number, boolean, or null. Scalars can be plain (unquoted), single-quoted (literal), or double-quoted (with escape sequences).

```yaml
plain: hello
quoted: "hello\nworld"
literal: 'hello'
```
**String** — A sequence of Unicode characters. Plain strings need no quotes. Double-quoted strings support escape sequences (`\n`, `\t`, `\\`). Single-quoted strings are literal.

```yaml
decimal: 42
octal: 0o52
hex: 0x2A
```
**Integer** — Whole number value. YAML supports decimal, octal (0o prefix), and hexadecimal (0x prefix) notations.

```yaml
fixed: 3.14
exponential: 3.14e+0
infinity: .inf
not_a_number: .nan
```
**Float** — Floating-point number. Supports fixed-point, exponential notation, positive/negative infinity (`.inf`/`-.inf`), and not-a-number (`.nan`).

```yaml
enabled: true
disabled: false
```
**Boolean** — Represents true or false. In YAML 1.2 core schema, only `true` and `false` are boolean. YAML 1.1 also recognized `yes`/`no`, `on`/`off`.

```yaml
explicit: null
tilde: ~
empty:
```
**Null** — Represents the absence of a value. Can be expressed as `null`, `~` (tilde), or by leaving the value empty after the colon.

```yaml
date: 2026-01-01
datetime: 2026-01-01T12:00:00Z
with_tz: 2026-01-01T12:00:00+05:30
```
**Timestamp** — Date and/or time in ISO 8601 format. Supports date-only, full datetime, and timezone offsets.

```yaml
icon: !!binary |
  R0lGODlhAQABAIAAAAAAAP///
```
**Binary** — Base64-encoded binary data using the `!!binary` tag. Used for embedding binary content like images or files in YAML.

```yaml
defaults: &defaults
  adapter: postgres
  host: localhost

production:
  <<: *defaults
  database: prod_db
```
**Anchor (`&`)** — Marks a node for later reuse. Define with `&name` and reference with `*name`. Avoids data duplication across the document.

```yaml
base: &base
  key: value

derived: *base
```
**Alias (`*`)** — References a previously anchored node. The alias resolves to the exact same value. Use with `&name` anchors for DRY configurations.

```yaml
defaults: &defaults
  timeout: 30
  retries: 3

service:
  <<: *defaults
  name: my-service
```
**Merge Key (`<<`)** — Merges key-value pairs from an anchored mapping into the current mapping. Local keys override merged ones. Supports merging multiple anchors: `<<: [*a, *b]`.

```yaml
---
first: document
---
second: document
```
**Document Start (`---`)** — Marks the beginning of a new YAML document. Required when a stream contains multiple documents. Optional for single-document files.

```yaml
key: value
...
```
**Document End (`...`)** — Marks the end of a YAML document without starting a new one. Signals that no more content follows.

```yaml
# This is a comment
key: value  # Inline comment
```
**Comment (`#`)** — Comments begin with `#` and continue to the end of the line. They are ignored by YAML parsers. Comments cannot appear inside scalars.

```yaml
script: |
  #!/bin/bash
  echo "hello"
  echo "world"
```
**Literal Block Scalar (`|`)** — Preserves newlines exactly as written. Each line break in the block becomes a literal `\n`. Ideal for scripts, code, or pre-formatted text.

```yaml
description: >
  This is a long
  description that
  will be folded.
```
**Folded Block Scalar (`>`)** — Folds newlines into spaces. Single newlines become spaces; blank lines become newlines. Useful for long text that should wrap.

```yaml
strip: |-
  no trailing newline
keep: |+
  trailing newlines kept


clip: |
  single trailing newline
```
**Chomp Indicators** — Control trailing newlines in block scalars:
- **Clip** (default, no indicator): Single trailing newline.
- **Strip (`-`)**: No trailing newlines (`|-`, `>-`).
- **Keep (`+`)**: All trailing newlines preserved (`|+`, `>+`).

```yaml
colors: [red, green, blue]
nested: [[1, 2], [3, 4]]
```
**Flow Sequence** — Compact inline syntax for sequences using square brackets `[]` with comma-separated values. Equivalent to the block style with `-` items.

```yaml
person: {name: John, age: 30}
```
**Flow Mapping** — Compact inline syntax for mappings using curly braces `{}` with comma-separated key-value pairs. Equivalent to the block style mapping.

```yaml
forced_string: !!str 123
forced_int: !!int "42"
forced_float: !!float "3.14"
```
**Tag (`!!type`)** — Explicitly specifies the data type of a value. Overrides automatic type resolution. Common tags: `!!str`, `!!int`, `!!float`, `!!bool`, `!!null`, `!!binary`, `!!seq`, `!!map`.

```yaml
? - key
  - part
: value

? {complex: key}
: value
```
**Complex Key (`?`)** — Allows non-simple keys such as sequences, mappings, or multi-line values as mapping keys. Use `?` to introduce the key and `:` for the value.

```yaml
%YAML 1.2
---
key: value
```
**%YAML Directive** — Specifies the YAML version for the document. Must appear before the document start marker `---`.

```yaml
%TAG !custom! tag:example.com,2026:
---
value: !custom!type data
```
**%TAG Directive** — Defines a shorthand prefix for tag URIs. Allows abbreviated tag notation throughout the document.

```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: my-app
  labels:
    app: my-app
spec:
  replicas: 3
  ...
```
**Kubernetes Resource** — Standard K8s manifest structure with `apiVersion`, `kind`, `metadata`, and `spec`. Every Kubernetes object follows this pattern.

```yaml
services:
  web:
    image: nginx:latest
    ports:
      - "8080:80"
    volumes:
      - ./html:/usr/share/nginx/html
```
**Docker Compose Service** — Defines a containerized service with image, port mappings, volumes, environment variables, networks, and dependency declarations.

```yaml
name: CI
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: npm test
```
**GitHub Actions Workflow** — Automates CI/CD with trigger events (`on`), jobs running on virtual machines, and steps that execute commands or reusable actions.

```yaml
strategy:
  matrix:
    os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest]
    node: [18, 20, 22]
  fail-fast: false
```
**GitHub Actions Matrix** — Runs a job across multiple configurations. Each combination of matrix values creates a separate job instance. Use `fail-fast` to control early termination.

```yaml
stages:
  - build
  - test
  - deploy

build_job:
  stage: build
  script:
    - make build
  artifacts:
    paths:
      - dist/
```
**CI/CD Pipeline** — Defines stages, jobs, scripts, artifacts, and caching for continuous integration and deployment. Common in GitLab CI, Azure Pipelines, and similar tools.

```yaml
# Literal (preserves newlines)
script: |
  line1
  line2

# Folded (joins lines)
text: >
  long
  paragraph

# Quoted (inline)
inline: "line1\nline2"
```
**Multi-line Strings** — YAML offers three approaches:
- **Literal `|`**: Preserves all newlines.
- **Folded `>`**: Joins lines with spaces.
- **Quoted**: Uses escape sequences like `\n`.

```yaml
parent:
  child:
    grandchild: value
```
**Indentation** — YAML uses spaces (not tabs) for indentation to denote structure. The number of spaces is flexible but must be consistent within a level. Two spaces is the most common convention.

```yaml
|- or >-
```
The strip chomping indicator '-' removes all trailing newlines from the block scalar. Used with literal '|-' or folded '>-' block scalars.

```yaml
|+ or >+
```
The keep chomping indicator '+' preserves all trailing newlines in the block scalar. Used with literal '|+' or folded '>+' block scalars.

```yaml
!!set { item1, item2 }
```
A YAML set is an unordered collection of unique values represented as a mapping with null values. Uses the !!set tag.

```yaml
!!omap [ {key: value} ]
```
A YAML ordered map is a sequence of key-value pairs where the order is significant. Uses the !!omap tag.

```yaml
services.<name>.image/build/ports/volumes
```
A Docker Compose service defines a container with its image, build config, port mappings, volume mounts, environment, networks, and dependencies.

```yaml
name/on/jobs
```
A GitHub Actions workflow defines automation with a name, trigger events (on), and a set of jobs each containing steps to execute.
